assumptions: The intragroup dividend has been recognised as a receivable by one group entity and a payable by another group entity.
The receivable/payable is a monetary item denominated in a currency relevant to foreign exchange risk.
The question concerns the consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: IAS 21 foreign currency accounting for monetary items
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes the baseline accounting for exchange differences on monetary items and specifically addresses intragroup monetary balances in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: IFRS 9 hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes the general IFRS 9 model for designating hedged items and expressly addresses when intragroup foreign currency exposures may qualify in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes the separate hedge accounting model for a net investment in a foreign operation, including related intragroup monetary items and presentation of exchange differences in consolidated financial statements.
